questions.text: In the preparation of a soap, the fatty acid containing less than eight carbon atoms is not used, because the soap obtained:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Forms a colloidal solution with water
options.explanation: This is not typically the case for soaps. Soap molecules form micelles in water, not colloidal solutions.
This is the characteristic of fatty acids with fewer than eight carrbon atoms. They tend to be insoluble in water and donot produce effective soaps. But, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Does not form an emulsion with the grease
options.explanation: Soap has tadpole like structure, because they posses long carboxylic acid chain through which hydrocarbons and sodium-pottasium ions are attached. Ions dissolves in water and non-ionic carbon chain dissolves in oil and form an emulsion but to form emulsion non-ionic chain must have more than 8 carbon atoms.

options.text: Forms an emulsion with the grease
options.explanation: This is not accurate for fatty acids with less than eight carbon atoms. They donot efficiently form emulsions with grease, making them less suitable for soap productiion.

questions.explanation: Soap has tadpole like structure, because they posses long carboxylic acid chain through which hydrocarbons and sodium-pottasium ions are attached. Ions dissolves in water and non-ionic carbon chain dissolves in oil and form an emulsion but to form emulsion non-ionic chain must have more than 8 carbon atoms.

questions.text: Which of the following processes is done to make the soap transparent?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Glycerol and gum are added to the soap.
options.explanation: Adding glycerol and gum to soap will not make it transparent. Glycerin is a clear liquid, but it is not a solvent for soap. Gum is a type of thickener, and it would only make the soap opaque.Hence, this is not the correct option.

options.text: The soap is dissolved in alcohol and then the alcohol is evaporated out.
options.explanation: To make the soap transparent firstly soap crystals are processed with solvents like glycerine, alcohol, and sugar to dissolve, allowing light to pass through it, then further the solvent is evaporated by heating the solution therefore soap becomes transparent.Hence, this is the correct answer.

options.text: Glycerine is added to the alcoholic solution of soap.
options.explanation: Adding glycerine to the alcoholic solution of soap will not make it transparent either. Glycerine is a clear liquid, but it is not a solvent for soap. It would only make the soap more viscous, but it would not make it transparent.Hence, this is not the correct option.

options.text: A saturated solution of sodium chloride is added to the soap.
options.explanation: Adding a saturated solution of sodium chloride to soap will not make it transparent. Sodium chloride is a white solid, and it would only make the soap opaque.

questions.explanation: To make the soap transparent firstly soap crystals are processed with solvents like glycerine, alcohol and sugar to dissolve, that allowing light to pass through it, then further the solvent is evaporated by heating the solution therefore soap becomes transparent.

questions.text: When soap or detergent is added to water, the surface tension of water is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Soap and detergent do not increase the surface tension of water, they reduce it. And soap and detergent do not have different effects on the surface tension of water, they both reduce it.

When soap and detergent are added to the water, the ionic part of soap and detergent attract grease and repel water molecules, thus the hydrogen bonds that hold the water molecules weaken therefore surface tension of water decreases.

options.text: Reduced by detergent and increased by soap
options.explanation: Soap and detergent do not increase the surface tension of water, they reduce it. And soap and detergent do not have different effects on the surface tension of water, they both reduce it.

options.text: Reduced by soap and increased by detergent
options.explanation: Soap and detergent do not increase the surface tension of water, they reduce it. And soap and detergent do not have different effects on the surface tension of water, they both reduce it.

questions.explanation: When soap and detergent are added to the water, the ionic part of soap and detergent attract grease and repel water molecules, thus the hydrogen bonds that hold the water molecules weaken therefore surface tension of water decreases.

questions.text: The saturated solution of sodium chloride is added in the hot process of preparation of soap, because:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: It increases the hardness of soap
options.explanation: The addition of sodium chloride does not directly affect the hardness of soap. Hardness in soap is typically associated with the presence of certain metal ions, not sodium chloride.

options.text: It increases the solubility of soap in water
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The addition of sodium chloride decreases the solubility of soap in water through the process of salting out, causing the soap to precipitate.

options.text: It decreases the solubility of soap in water
options.explanation: When a saturated solution of sodium chloride is added during the hot process of soap preparation, it is known as salting out. The addition of sodium chloride reduces the solubility of soap in water, causing the soap to precipitate out of the solution. This helps in the separation of the soap from the reaction mixture.

options.text: It decreases the surface tension of water
options.explanation: The addition of sodium chloride primarily influences the solubility of soap, not the surface tension of water. The reduction in surface tension is usually associated with the addition of surfactants like soap itself, not sodium chloride.

questions.explanation: Solubility of soap in water depends upon the non-ionic or carbohydrate part of soap, as the sodium chloride is added in the hot process of preparation of soap the ionic part of soap increases therefore solubility of soap in water decreases.When a saturated solution of sodium chloride is added during the hot process of soap preparation, it is known as salting out. The addition of sodium chloride reduces the solubility of soap in water, causing the soap to precipitate out of the solution. This helps in the separation of the soap from the reaction mixture.

questions.text: Which of the following statement is true?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Soaps and detergents both are used only in soft water
options.explanation: As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Soaps and detergents both are used in soft as well as hard water.
options.explanation: As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Soap is used only in soft water while detergent can be used in soft as well as hard water
options.explanation: Sodium-potassium ions that are present in ionic part of soap forms scum with calcium and magnesium ions of hard water and get precipitated whereas calcium and magnesium salts of detergent are soluble in water. Thus soaps tend to form scum in hard water due to the reaction between soap molecules and calcium or magnesium ions, which are commonly present in hard water. On the other hand, detergents are synthetic and are designed to work effectively in both hard and soft water. As a result soap forms lather only in soft water whereas detergent forms lather in both soft and hard water.

options.text: Detergent is used only in soft water while soap is used in soft and hard water both
options.explanation: As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Sodium-potassium ions that are present in ionic part of soap forms scum with calcium and magnesium ions of hard water and get precipitated whereas calcium and magnesium salts of detergent are soluble in water. Thus soaps tend to form scum in hard water due to the reaction between soap molecules and calcium or magnesium ions, which are commonly present in . hard water. On the other hand, detergents are synthetic and are designed to work effectively in both hard and soft water. As a result soap forms lather only in soft water whereas detergent forms lather in both soft and hard water. Thus, option C is correct.

questions.text: What is the type of particle formed after the contact of sodium stearate molecules with grease particle?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.

Negatively charged particles are formed after the contact of sodium stearate molecules with grease particle. When sodium stearate molecules come into contact with grease particles, they form structures known as micelles. Micelles are colloidal particles that have a hydrophilic (water-attracting) outer surface and a hydrophobic (water-repelling) interior. In a micelle, the hydrophilic (charged) heads of the sodium stearate molecules face outward, creating a negatively charged outer surface. The hydrophobic tails are oriented toward the interior, where the grease or oil molecules are trapped.
As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Positively charged from inside and negatively charged from outside
options.explanation: As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Negatively charged particles are formed after the contact of sodium stearate molecules with grease particle. When sodium stearate molecules come into contact with grease particles, they form structures known as micelles. Micelles are colloidal particles that have a hydrophilic (water-attracting) outer surface and a hydrophobic (water-repelling) interior. In a micelle, the hydrophilic (charged) heads of the sodium stearate molecules face outward, creating a negatively charged outer surface. The hydrophobic tails are oriented toward the interior, where the grease or oil molecules are trapped. Thus, option B is correct.

questions.text: Which of the following compounds is used in the synthesis of a parent compound of tranquillizers and sedative medicines by condensation with urea?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: As per the explanation, this option is incotrrect. Barbituric acid is the parent compound of tranquilizers.
As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.

Barbituric acid (tranquilizer) is formed by condensation of melonic ester with urea in presence of sodium ethoxide. The compound used in the synthesis of the parent compound of tranquilizers and sedative medicines by condensation with urea is indeed malonic ester. The process involves the condensation of malonic ester with urea to form a barbituric acid derivative, which serves as the parent compound for various barbiturate drugs.
As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Barbituric acid (tranquilizer) is formed by condensation of melonic ester with urea in presence of sodium ethoxide. The compound used in the synthesis of the parent compound of tranquilizers and sedative medicines by condensation with urea is indeed malonic ester (option c). The process involves the condensation of malonic ester with urea to form a barbituric acid derivative, which serves as the parent compound for various barbiturate drugs.

questions.text: Choose the fever reducing drug?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Sedatives are substances, typically drugs or medications, that have a calming or tranquilizing effect on the nervous system. They are used to induce relaxation, alleviate anxiety, and promote sleep by depressing the activity of the central nervous system.

Antiseptics are substances that are applied to living tissues or skin to prevent or inhibit the growth of microorganisms, such as bacteria, viruses, and fungi. They are commonly used to disinfect wounds, cuts, and other external injuries.
Antipyretic is fever reducing drug. Antipyretics enable the hypothalamus to control temperature changes that are caused by prostaglandin.

Antidepressants are a class of medications primarily used to treat mood disorders, particularly major depressive disorder and related conditions. They work by altering the balance of certain chemicals in the brain, such as serotonin, norepinephrine, and dopamine, which are thought to be involved in regulating mood.

questions.explanation: Antipyretic is fever reducing drug. Antipyretics enable the hypothalamus to control temperature changes that are caused by prostaglandin.

questions.text: Hydrolysis of an oil by caustic alkali is called?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Esterification is the process of forming an ester from a reaction between a carboxylic acid and an alcohol. It is the opposite of hydrolysis.

In saponification oil is hydrolysed by caustic alkali in to alcohol and soap. Saponification involves the reaction between triglycerides (fats/oils) and a strong base (caustic alkali) to produce soap and glycerol.
Acetylation involves the addition of an acetyl group (CH3CO-) to a molecule. It is a specific type of chemical reaction where an acetyl group is introduced into a compound.
Carboxylation is the addition of a carboxyl group (COOH) to a compound. This term is often associated with biochemical processes where a carboxyl group is added to a molecule.

questions.explanation: In saponification oil is hydrolysed by caustic alkali in to alcohol and soap. Saponification involves the reaction between triglycerides (fats/oils) and a strong base (caustic alkali) to produce soap and glycerol.

questions.text: Soaps and detergents are kept in the family of which of the following types of compounds?

questions.text: The substance which affect the central nervous system and induce sleep are called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Antipyretics are drugs or substances that are used to reduce fever. They work by lowering the body temperature, often by inhibiting the production of certain chemicals called prostaglandins that are involved in the regulation of body temperature.

Tranquilizers are designed to treat anxiety, fear, tension etc by inducing sleep through central nervous system. Valium (diazepam), ativan (lorazepam), xanax (alprazolam) are some examples of tranquilizers.

Analgesics are medications or substances that are used to relieve pain without causing a loss of consciousness. They work by blocking pain signals or reducing the perception of pain in the central nervous system.
As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Tranquilizers are designed to treat anxiety, fear, tension etc by inducing sleep through central nervous system. Valium (diazepam), ativan (lorazepam), xanax (alprazolam) are some examples of tranquilizers.

options.explanation: As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.

Synthetic antibiotics that contain sulfanilamide molecular structure are known as sulfonamide or sulpha drugs. Sulfa medicines were the first widely used chemical agents to treat and prevent human bacterial infections.
